summary: UR273B is the AUDIT-TOTALS HALF of the UR273A/B INSEPARABLE COMPOUND DOCUMENT at Incahuasi Sector A Subsector 1 UA 4 Unidad 20N UE 14. 124 cords, 1-PAIRE architecture (schema CALCULATOR), 32 clusters (avg 3.6 cords). CRITICAL FINDING: the INT cords carry HUGE VALUES (top: 3991, 2300, 2142, 1662, 1575, 1370 x2, 1361, 1334, 1332) summing to 29,317 -- radically larger than UR273A's scattered small values (30-891). This confirms the v4-UR273A hypothesis: UR273A holds the sparse EVENT LOG (7/8 cadastral markers compressed p40-p63, scattered small numbers); UR273B holds the AUDIT TOTALS for events logged against UR273A's cadastral identity. KFG twin KH0511 catalogued but PENDANT-PENDANT SUMS PAGE 404 (same as UR273A) -- no Ascher layer. Quality: AGREED=15 + KFG_RESOLVED=17 + OKR_ONLY=92; OKR/KFG agreement 46.9 percent. 3 STRING at 100 percent dict hit: qaqa (p14 W rock landmark) + chiqa (p91 W verification) + taqa (p103 W boundary) = 3 of 8 canonical cadastral markers, DISTRIBUTED ACROSS THE DOCUMENT rather than compressed (UR273A compresses 7/8 into 23 cord-positions; UR273B spreads 3/8 across 103 cord-positions). Mode B checkbox dating: month 2 (July, Chahua Huarquiz). 73 percent empty cords (91/124). 5 colors: W(53 43%) + NB(34 27%) + AB(17 14%) + KB(8) + other minor. Same OKR physical note as UR273A: 'Two khipus tied to each other by cords on both primary cords' -- STRONGEST DUAL-BINDING in the Incahuasi set, cord 1 = SINGLE COMPOUND CORD shared with UR273A.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION
  124 cords. 30 INT, 3 STRING (100 percent dict), 91 EMPTY (73 percent).
  Architecture: 1-PAIRE, schema CALCULATOR (depth L2 available but 114 L1 + 0 L2 in practice).
  32 clusters, avg 3.6 cords, range 1-7.
  Colors: W(53, 43%), NB(34, 27%), AB(17, 14%), KB(8), 0G(3), 0B(2), MB(2), ?(5).
  Provenance: Sector A Subsector 1 UA 4 Unidad 20N UE 14 -- SAME UE as UR273A.
  Museum: Proyecto Incahuasi.
  OKR note: 'See notes on UR273A' = physically joined to UR273A.
  Date: MODE B checkbox -- month 2 tick = July (Chahua Huarquiz, Inca calendar month 1 = June).

DATA QUALITY (v3)
  KFG twin KH0511 catalogued; pendant-pendant sums page returns 404 (NO ASCHER LAYER -- same as UR273A).
  Quality distribution: AGREED=15 + KFG_RESOLVED=17 + OKR_ONLY=92 = 124 total.
  OKR/KFG agreement 46.9 percent.
  v1 -> v3: 17 L? cords resolved via KFG.

ASCHER LAYER
  NOT APPLICABLE. KFG pendant-pendant sum page 404 for KH0511.
  0 Ascher sums. No Pass 3A/3C/3D possible.
  Consistent with the audit-totals function: the totals THEMSELVES are the verification; they do
  not require additional pendant-pendant checksum structure.

VOCABULARY (3 STRING, 100 percent dict hit)
  qaqa  x1 (p14 W)  -- ROCK / LANDMARK
  chiqa x1 (p91 W)  -- TRUTH / VERIFICATION
  taqa  x1 (p103 W) -- BOUNDARY
  3 of 8 canonical cadastral markers, DISTRIBUTED ACROSS the document (p14 opening, p91 middle,
  p103 late) rather than COMPRESSED into a single block (as UR273A does at p40-p63).
  Opening -> middle -> late cadastral anchors = scaffolding for the audit totals.

INT VALUES -- HUGE AUDIT TOTALS
  Top 10 INT (all W): 3991, 2300, 2142, 1662, 1575, 1370 x2, 1361, 1334, 1332.
  Total sum across 30 INT: 29,317.
  MEAN per filled cord: 29,317 / 30 = 977.
  Order of magnitude: 100-1000x UR273A's scattered values (30-891).
  Signature: AUDIT-SCALE TOTALS -- the quantitative complement to UR273A's small-event register.

DUAL-BINDING
  UR273A and UR273B are tied at BOTH PRIMARY CORDS by KB + NB-W double-wrap with shared subsidiary
  (OKR). Cord 1 of each khipu IS a single compound cord.
  This is the STRONGEST physical binding in the Incahuasi set -- stronger than UR267A/B (beginning
  only) or UR255/256.

HYPOTHESIS
  UR273B is the AUDIT TOTALS counterpart to UR273A's sparse event log. The v4-UR273A deep-read
  proposed: 'UR273A holds the sparse event log; UR273B likely carries the audit totals for events
  logged against the cadastral identity established at p40-p63.' This hypothesis is CONFIRMED by the
  UR273B structural signature: 30 INT cords with values 100-1000x larger than UR273A's small
  scattered numbers, summing to 29,317 across a 73 percent empty register. The 3 cadastral markers
  (qaqa + chiqa + taqa) distributed across the document provide scaffolding anchors tying the
  audit totals to UR273A's compressed cadastral identity block.
  UR273A is the transaction-level detail; UR273B is the summary. Together they form the Incahuasi
  UE 14 COMPOUND LEDGER for July of an unspecified Inca year.

COLOR ROLE MAP
  W (53, 43%)  = PRIMARY AUDIT CHANNEL -- carries ALL 3 STRING cords + ALL largest INT totals.
  NB (34, 27%) = STRUCTURAL CHANNEL (complementary to UR273A's NB-dominant structural signature).
  AB (17, 14%) = SECONDARY DATA.
  KB (8),  0G (3), 0B (2), MB (2) = minor.

KEY PASSAGES
  p1: SHARED COMPOUND CORD with UR273A (KB + NB-W double-wrap).
  p2: W INT=3991 = HIGHEST AUDIT TOTAL in the document.
  p14: W qaqa = first cadastral anchor (rock landmark).
  p91: W chiqa = mid-document verification anchor.
  p103: W taqa = late-document boundary anchor.
  Mode B month-2 tick cord: July (Chahua Huarquiz).
narrative:
Three thousand nine hundred and ninety-one. That is the first non-zero number in UR273B, at cord two, on white. The largest total in the document. In the compound khipu UR273A-plus-UR273B, where cord one is a single shared compound cord physically binding both documents at both ends, UR273B opens with its loudest number.

Thirty numerical cords carry the audit. Three thousand nine hundred and ninety-one, two thousand three hundred, two thousand one hundred and forty-two, one thousand six hundred and sixty-two, one thousand five hundred and seventy-five, one thousand three hundred and seventy twice, one thousand three hundred and sixty-one, one thousand three hundred and thirty-four, one thousand three hundred and thirty-two. Each total on white thread, each at an order of magnitude that UR273A never approached. UR273A's scattered values topped out at eight hundred and ninety-one; UR273B's smallest top-ten total is one thousand three hundred and thirty-two. The same kind of register would not produce this gap. UR273B is a different KIND of document.

UR273A compressed the complete parcel-naming formula into twenty-three cord positions at its centre -- waqa, qaqa, chaqa, kaqa, chiqa, taqa, paqa -- seven of the eight canonical cadastral markers stacked at three-cord cadence. UR273B does something different. Three cadastral markers only -- qaqa at cord fourteen, chiqa at cord ninety-one, taqa at cord one hundred and three. SPREAD across the whole document. Opening, middle, late. Scaffolding anchors rather than a compressed identity block. The document is not naming the parcel; it is counting what the parcel produced.

And the Mode B tick -- a single cord at position two of the first twelve L1 positions carrying value ten -- dates the audit to month two of the Inca calendar. Inca month one is June. Month two is July. Chahua Huarquiz. The July audit.

Seventy-three percent of UR273B is empty. Ninety-one cords of one hundred and twenty-four carry no knots. This is a PRE-FORMATTED audit sheet with thirty filled totals, three lexical anchors, and ninety-one prepared-but-unused positions. Like UR192 at Huaca Perez, like UR273A at fifty-four and seventy-four percent respectively. A register that accepted some entries and left the rest blank until the next reporting cycle.

The KFG catalog did not publish a pendant-pendant sums page for KH0511. Same silence as for UR273A. The two khipus together reach the limit of the Ascher framework; their arithmetic verification, if it ever existed, did not survive into the digital indexes. What remains is the compound physical object: two khipus tied at both primary cords by a KB cord and a NB-W cord wrapping both primaries with a shared subsidiary. Cord one of UR273A is cord one of UR273B. They cannot be separated without cutting the binding.

And what UR273B says to UR273A, cord by cord, is this: you named the parcel -- I counted the delivery. You wrote waqa and qaqa and chaqa and kaqa and chiqa and taqa and paqa into your twenty-three-cord central block; I wrote three thousand nine hundred and ninety-one into my second cord. The parcel was. The tribute came. July. The Incahuasi UE 14 compound ledger for one named Inca year, paired and bound and half-used, preserved together in the storeroom at Sector A Subsector 1 for five centuries until the excavation notebook entry marked 2014.

prior_work.claim: UR273A + UR273B constitute a dual-bound inseparable compound khipu at Incahuasi Sector A UE 14, tied at BOTH primary cords by KB + NB-W wraps with shared subsidiary -- strongest dual binding in the Incahuasi excavation.
prior_work.citation: Urton, G. and Chu, A. (2015). Accounting in the King's Storehouse: the Inkawasi Khipu Archive. LAQ 26(4); OKR cord-note for UR273A.

prior_work.claim: Chu excavation records place UR273A + UR273B in Sector A Subsector 1 UA 4 Unidad 20N UE 14 -- the main Incahuasi archive block, adjacent to UR266, UR270, UR278, UR268, UR272, UR275, UR277 at the same storeroom complex.
prior_work.citation: Chu, A. (2013+). Proyecto Arqueologico Incahuasi excavation reports.

prior_work.claim: Canonical Andean cadastral vocabulary (waqa, qaqa, chaqa, kaqa, chiqa, taqa, paqa, siqa) marks parcel-naming registers across the OKR cadastral sub-corpus; UR273A compresses 7/8 markers at p40-p63.
prior_work.citation: Sivan, J. (2026). Cadastral vocabulary corpus analysis; Gonzalez Holguin, D. (1608). Vocabulario.

v3_contribution.claim: UR273A/B PAIRED-DOCUMENT HYPOTHESIS CONFIRMED: the v4-UR273A deep-read predicted that UR273B would carry 'the audit totals for events logged against the cadastral identity established at p40-p63.' UR273B's 30 INT cords summing to 29,317 with top values 3991 / 2300 / 2142 / 1662 -- 100-1000x UR273A's small scattered values -- confirm the audit-totals function.

evidence.test: top-10 INT value comparison UR273A vs UR273B (factor 100-1000x)

v3_contribution.claim: DISTRIBUTED-vs-COMPRESSED CADASTRAL SCAFFOLDING: UR273A compresses 7/8 cadastral markers into 23 cord-positions (p40-p63, 3-cord cadence); UR273B spreads 3/8 markers across 103 cord-positions (qaqa p14, chiqa p91, taqa p103). Opening-middle-late scaffolding anchors tying the audit totals to UR273A's compressed identity block.

evidence.test: cadastral-marker positional distribution UR273A vs UR273B

v3_contribution.claim: MODE B MONTH 2 DATING: single tick cord at position 2 of first 12 L1 cords = July (Chahua Huarquiz, Inca calendar month 1 = June). The July audit of an unspecified Inca year.

AUDIT-TOTALS SUB-TYPE IDENTIFIED: 3-STRING cadastral-anchor + 30 huge-INT totals + 73 percent empty = AUDIT TOTALS document class. Distinct from day-book (UR270, UR267A), audit-integrity (UR267B, UR256, UR275), and district-summary (UR268) sub-types of commodity_accounting. UR273B is the first documented audit-TOTALS exemplar in the analysed Incahuasi corpus.

evidence.test: audit-totals signature vs other commodity sub-types

v3_contribution.claim: NO ASCHER LAYER on KH0511 (pendant-pendant sums page 404) parallels UR273A's missing Ascher layer. The compound-document pair sits outside the KFG arithmetic-catalog framework despite both documents being KFG-catalogued at knot-level. Joint silence = structural feature of the event-log + audit-totals pair.

evidence.test: KFG pendant-pendant sums 404 on both KH0510 and KH0511

companions.note: PHYSICALLY INSEPARABLE COMPOUND DOCUMENT. UR273A (87 cords, 74 percent empty, 7/8 cadastral markers compressed p40-p63, scattered small INT) + UR273B (this document, 124 cords, 73 percent empty, 3/8 markers distributed, 30 huge INT audit totals sum=29,317). Cord 1 of each IS a single shared compound cord. UR273A = event log; UR273B = audit totals.

evidence.test: OKR both-ends KB + NB-W double-wrap binding

companions.note: UE 12 cross-khipu audit of UR278 (peanut recap, 40-cord row-totals null p<1e-5). UR266 and UR273B are both AUDIT-tier documents but at different scopes: UR266 recaps another whole khipu; UR273B totals its compound partner's events.
UE 16 aji tier-2 AUDIT (8/10 Ascher verified, chiqa-chiqa doubled seal, 10-constant). Both audit-function but UR267B has arithmetic-verification-grade Ascher layer while UR273B lacks it (KFG 404).
UE 19 peanut audit (47-constant, 5/5 Ascher verified). Cadastral-anchored audit parallel. UR275 uses paqa-taqa-kaqa anchoring similar to UR273B's qaqa-chiqa-taqa but with Ascher verification.
UE 1 'Quipu del Mani' peanut tier-1 day-book. Same site + adjacent UE. Day-book counterpart to UR273A/B's event-log + audit-totals pair.
UE 18 peanut tier-3 district summary (presentation binding + red tassel). Different tier; same archival complex.

Huaca Perez tambo accounting (70 cords, 54 percent empty, 0 STRING). UR273B's 73 percent empty + 3 STRING = higher-empty-rate + low-lexical-density variant of the pre-formatted administrative-register convention.

word_reading.note: 3/3 STRING at 100 percent dict hit. qaqa, chiqa, taqa all dictionary-stable core cadastral markers.

document_type.note: audit_totals sub-type supported by (a) huge INT values 100-1000x UR273A's scattered numbers, (b) 3-cadastral-marker scaffolding distributed across the document, (c) Mode B July dating anchor, (d) physical dual-binding with UR273A establishing complement-function. New sub-type; not cross-validated against another audit-totals-only exemplar.

narrative.note: The 'July audit totals for UR273A's event log' reading is internally coherent and directly anchored on (a) huge-INT signature vs UR273A's small-INT signature, (b) distributed cadastral scaffolding as audit anchors vs UR273A's compressed cadastral identity block, (c) physical dual-binding establishing the pair as a single ledger. Confirms v4-UR273A hypothesis.

data_integrity.note: NO ASCHER LAYER (KH0511 pendant-pendant sums page 404). OKR/KFG agreement 46.9 percent -- moderate but not DIVERGENT-flagged. 17 L? cords resolved via KFG merge. The arithmetic verification that an audit document normally carries is not available; the totals themselves must be trusted on the basis of the paired-document context.

sections.title: Opening -- Shared Cord + First Audit Total
sections.reading: Cord 1 = SHARED COMPOUND CORD with UR273A (KB + NB-W double-wrap binding at both primary cords). Cord 2 W INT=3991 = HIGHEST AUDIT TOTAL of the document. Opening establishes the dual-binding + first large total.

key_cords.note: DUAL-BINDING with UR273A via KB + NB-W double-wrap
HIGHEST AUDIT TOTAL in the document (first non-zero entry)

sections.title: First Cadastral Anchor
sections.reading: p14 W qaqa = ROCK LANDMARK -- first of 3 distributed cadastral scaffolding anchors. Ties the audit totals to UR273A's compressed parcel-identity block (which opens on waqa at p40).

sections.title: Main Audit-Totals Block
sections.reading: Body of the audit. 28 remaining INT cords on W carry the July totals. Top values 2300, 2142, 1662, 1575, 1370 x2, 1361, 1334, 1332 distributed across the block. Bulk of the 29,317 document-total sum lies here.

sections.title: Mid-Document Verification Anchor
sections.reading: p91 W chiqa = VERIFICATION / TRUTH. Second of 3 cadastral scaffolding anchors. Certifies the audit at the 75 percent point of the document.

sections.title: Late Boundary Anchor + Closing
sections.reading: p101 W INT=1662 is the largest late-block total. p103 W taqa = BOUNDARY (3rd and final cadastral anchor). Remaining cords tail off into the 73 percent empty prepared-but-unused positions.

key_cords.note: closes the compound ledger's audit half

references: Sivan, J. (2026). The Khipu as a Layered Information System. ALBA Project preprint.

OKR notes on UR273A (inherited): 'Two Khipus tied to each other by cords on both primary cords. Both Beginnings tied by KB cord + NB-W cord across both PCs.'

Compare UR273A (paired event-log half), UR266 (UE 12 cross-khipu peanut audit), UR267B + UR275 (Incahuasi audit-tier with Ascher), UR270 + UR268 (day-book + district-summary tiers), UR278 (UE 16 commodity matrix), UR192 (pre-formatted register convention).
